title: dsVertClient
text: dsVertClient provides user-friendly R functions privacy-preserving analysis vertically partitioned data across DataSHIELD servers. analyst calls simple functions; cryptographic protocols (ECDH-PSI, Ring63 / Ring127 Beaver MPC, DCF wide-spline, OT-Beaver dishonest-majority triples, X25519 + AES-256-GCM transport, Ed25519 identity verification) run transparently. Pair server-side companion package dsVert.

title: dsVertClient
code: library(DSI); library(DSOpal); library(dsVertClient)  # Connect to servers conns <- DSI::datashield.login(logins = builder$build()) DSI::datashield.assign.table(conns, "D", "project.data")  # 1. Align records (NAs removed automatically, like glm na.omit) ds.psiAlign("D", "patient_id", "DA", datasources = conns)  # 2. Fit a federated GLM (auto-detects which server has each variable) fit <- ds.vertGLM(diabetes ~ age + bmi + glu + bp,                   data = "DA", family = "binomial",                   datasources = conns) print(fit)  # 3. Cox PH on K=2 with Ring127 (default; STRICT-grade vs survival::coxph) cox <- ds.vertCox(survival::Surv(time, event) ~ age + bmi + bp,                   data = "DA", compute_se = TRUE,                   datasources = conns)  # 4. Correlation + PCA cor <- ds.vertCor("DA", datasources = conns) pca <- ds.vertPCA(cor_result = cor)  DSI::datashield.logout(conns)

title: Adaptive Chunking for DataSHIELD Transport — chunk-utils
text: DataSHIELD passes function arguments inline R expressions via HTTP. Large string arguments (base64-encoded ciphertexts, EC points, encrypted vectors) can exceed parser HTTP body size limit. module implements adaptive chunking: Start large initial chunk size (default 100KB, configurable via options(dsvert.chunk_size = N)) first large send, probe whether chunk size accepted probe fails, reduce chunk size 25\ Cache successful chunk size subsequent sends probe conclusive chunk least half target chunk size successfully sent. Small payloads succeed trivially confirm full chunk size accepted.

title: One-step Newton Cox at beta=0 (client orchestrator) — .ds_vertCox_newton_one_step
text: Called ds.vertCox one_step_newton = TRUE. Pre-conditions (caller already:) * standardised X via .glm_mpc_setup() * shared X (k2ShareInputDS / k2ReceiveShareDS) * registered Cox times (k2SetCoxTimesDS / k2ReceiveCoxMetaDS) * applied permutation (k2ApplyCoxPermutationDS) ss$k2_x_share_fp, ss$k2_peer_x_share_fp, ss$k2_cox_delta_fp sorted time parties. : 1. dsvertCoxNewtonPrepDS parties (local extract + cumsum). 2. dsvertCoxNewtonGradDS parties -> aggregate grad vector. 3. Fisher pair (j <= k), run two Beaver vecmul protocols (one XX term, one SS term), scalar aggregate. 4. Solve beta = solve(Fisher, grad). Returns std-scale beta.

title: Cox Path B client orchestrator — .ds_vertCox_path_b_fisher
text: Iterative Newton Fisher(beta_k) via Beaver session-live mu, G, S shares. See docs/error_bounds/cox_newton_onestep.md docs/acceptance/path_b_targets.md acceptance gates govern code. Pre-conditions (Newton one-step): - standardised X shared (k2ShareInputDS) - Cox times + delta broadcast (k2SetCoxTimesDS / k2ReceiveCoxMetaDS) - permutation applied (k2ApplyCoxPermutationDS) - one-step Newton prep populated cox_n_Xc__fp per canonical j - one-step Newton fit produced initial beta_1 caller Per iter: 1 call .cox_score_round (populates mu, G, S shares) + (p) Beaver vecmuls X_j * mu +1 Beaver vecmul 1/S^2 + 2 * p(p+1)/2 Beaver vecmuls Term1 (X*X muG) + 2 * p(p+1)/2 Beaver vecmuls Term2 (TT 1/S^2) = p + 1 + 2p(p+1) Beaver vecmuls. p=5: 5 + 1 + 60 = 66 Beaver vecmuls per iter. Max 5 iters per P3 budget.

title: LMM closed-form GLS driver — .ds_vertLMM_closed_form
text: Computes exact Laird-Ware generalised-least-squares estimate random-intercept LMM without relying ds.vertGLM's L-BFGS iteration. Directly assembles Gram matrix X'X right-hand side X'y cluster-mean- centred design via: - local per-server block computations - Beaver vecmul + FP-sum cross-server entries solves beta = solve(XtX, Xty) client-side. Matches lme4::lmer FP precision (~1e-5 small n). Assumptions: - Cluster IDs broadcast via dsvertLMMBroadcastClusterIDsDS / dsvertLMMReceiveClusterIDsDS. - Transport keys initialised session. - Exactly K=2 servers (outcome + one peer).

text: function performs privacy-preserving record alignment single call, using ECDH-PSI blind-relay transport encryption.

title: ECDH-PSI Record Alignment (Blind Relay) — ds.psiAlign
text: ECDH-PSI exploits commutativity elliptic curve scalar multiplication: \(\alpha \cdot (\beta \cdot H(id)) = \beta \cdot (\alpha \cdot H(id))\). EC point exchanges encrypted server--server (X25519 + AES-256-GCM ECIES). client acts blind relay, seeing opaque blobs. Phase 0: server generates X25519 transport keypair. Public keys exchanged via client. Phase 1: reference server masks IDs scalar \(\alpha\). Points stored server-side (returned client). target server: reference encrypts masked points target's PK. target decrypts, generates scalar \(\beta\), double-masks ref points (stores locally), masks IDs, encrypts ref's PK. reference decrypts, double-masks \(\alpha\), encrypts result target's PK. target decrypts, matches double-masked sets, aligns data. multi-server intersection ensures records present servers retained.

what: Security (DDH assumption on P-256, malicious-client model)

title: Federated Cox proportional-hazards regression — ds.vertCox
text: Fit Cox PH model vertically partitioned DataSHIELD data using reverse-cumsum reformulation partial- likelihood score. client runs L-BFGS outer loop; step obtains aggregate gradient $$\nabla \ell(\beta) = \sum_j x_j (\delta_j - e^{\eta_j} G_j)$$ $$G_j = \sum_{: \delta_i=1, t_i \le t_j} 1 / S(t_i)$$ $$S(t_i) = \sum_{k: t_k \ge t_i} e^{\eta_k}$$ via already-deployed server helpers (k2SetCoxTimesDS / k2ApplyCoxPermutationDS / k2CoxReverseCumsumSDS / k2StoreCoxRecipDS / k2CoxForwardCumsumGDS) existing 4-phase DCF protocol family="exp" + family="reciprocal". partial-likelihood Beaver matvec handled glmRing63GenGradTriplesDS / k2StoreGradTripleDS / k2GradientR1DS / k2GradientR2DS machinery ds.vertGLM uses, new cryptographic round introduced. Client view per iteration: p-dimensional aggregate gradient scalar partial log-likelihood (via standard Beaver-sum path). client never sees \(\eta_j\), \(S(t_j)\), \(G_j\), per-patient quantity. Inter-server disclosure: DCF peer learns ascending-time sort permutation (ranking event times) binary event indicator. Absolute event times disclosed.

what: Federated binomial GLMM via Laplace approximation — ds.vertGLMM
title: Federated binomial GLMM via Laplace approximation — ds.vertGLMM
text: Fit binomial generalised linear mixed model single random intercept vertically partitioned DataSHIELD data via Laplace-approximated marginal likelihood: $$\ell_L(\beta, \sigma_b^2) = \sum_i \left[\ell_i(\beta, b_i^*)         - \tfrac{1}{2} \log |H_i(b_i^*)| \right]$$ \(b_i^*\) mode per-cluster penalised log-likelihood \(\ell_i(\beta, b) = \sum_j \log f(y_{ij}|   x_{ij}^T \beta + b) - b^2/(2\sigma_b^2)\) \(H_i\) Hessian mode. Architecture: cluster (cluster IDs outcome server) INNER L-BFGS \(b_i\) executed server-side (using cached dsvertClusterResidualsDS aggregates + cached \(\eta_i\) share ds.vertGLM(keep_session=TRUE)). outer optimiser \((\beta, \sigma_b^2)\) client-side, re-calling ds.vertGLM shrinkage- weight column derived current \(b_i^*\). Variance-component update uses moment-matching estimate \(\hat\sigma_b^2 = \mathrm{var}(\hat b_i)\) across clusters (Breslow-Clayton approximation). scaffolding reuses every primitive already shipped dsVert 1.1.0+: per-cluster residual aggregates, keep_session flag ds.vertGLM, Beaver vecmul inner weighted updates, DCF sigmoid/exp wide-splines. Privacy: client sees (beta, sigma_b^2) + per-cluster b_hat_i estimates aggregate vector (one value per cluster). per-patient quantity ever leaves DCF parties. Inter-server leakage: cluster membership (tier ds.vertLMM).

what: Iterative proximal-gradient LASSO over the MPC GLM gradient — ds.vertLASSOIter
title: Iterative proximal-gradient LASSO over the MPC GLM gradient — ds.vertLASSOIter
text: Proper proximal-gradient L1-regularised GLM fitting invokes full MPC gradient pipeline outer step. iteration \(t\): $$\nabla_t = \mathrm{ds.vertGLM}(\beta_t)$$ $$\beta_{t+1} = \mathrm{soft}(\beta_t - \alpha \nabla_t, \alpha \lambda)$$ \(\alpha\) backtracking step size chosen guarantee descent smooth part objective, soft-threshold operator enforces L1 sparsity pattern. intercept penalised. Unlike ds.vertLASSO1Step (post-hoc soft-thresholds converged GLM solution via local quadratic surrogate around \(\hat\beta\)), routine re-evaluates gradient current sparse iterate \(\beta_t\), final estimate true proximal-gradient L1 solution rather one-step surrogate. Costs M + 1 MPC GLM gradient invocations M iter. Non-disclosure: inner ds.vertGLM call already hides everything p-aggregate level; outer loop manipulates returned \(p\)-vector coefficients.

what: Proper LASSO via client-side proximal gradient on normal equations — ds.vertLASSOProximal
title: Proper LASSO via client-side proximal gradient on normal equations — ds.vertLASSOProximal
text: Cierra el gap OLS-soft-threshold -> proper-LASSO usando solo cantidades ya expuestas por ds.vertGLM (beta, covariance, n), sin anadir ninguna ronda MPC adicional. Normal equations: para Gaussian \(y = X\beta + \varepsilon\) el minimizador LASSO es $$\beta^* = \arg\min_{\beta} \tfrac{1}{2n} \|y - X\beta\|^2 + \lambda \|\beta\|_1.$$ El gradiente de la parte cuadratica es $$\nabla f(\beta) = \tfrac{1}{n} X^\top X \cdot (\beta - \hat\beta_{OLS}).$$ ds.vertGLM ya expone \(\hat\beta_{OLS}\) y \(\mathrm{Cov}(\hat\beta) = \sigma^2 (X^\top X)^{-1}\). De ahi el cliente reconstruye \(X^\top X / n = \mathrm{Cov}^{-1} \cdot \hat\sigma^2 / n\) e itera proximal-gradient puramente en memoria: \(\beta_{t+1} = S_{\lambda/L}(\beta_t - \eta \nabla f(\beta_t))\) con \(L \ge \lambda_{\max}(X^\top X / n)\) (upper bound local) y \(S_t(x) = \mathrm{sign}(x) \max(|x|-t, 0)\) el operador soft-threshold.

what: Federated linear mixed model with a single random intercept — ds.vertLMM
title: Federated linear mixed model with a single random intercept — ds.vertLMM
text: Fit random-intercept linear mixed model \(y_{ij} = x_{ij}^T \beta + b_i + \varepsilon_{ij}\) vertically partitioned DataSHIELD data, cluster indicator id lives outcome server. REML log-likelihood profile expressed terms single variance ratio \(\rho = \sigma_b^2 / (\sigma^2 + n_i\sigma_b^2)\) outer optimiser one-dimensional. outer step calls ds.vertGLM per-patient weights derived current \(\rho\), reusing already-deployed ds.vertGLM(weights=) infrastructure. Derivation (Laird-Ware compact form): $$V_i = \sigma^2 + \sigma_b^2 \mathbf{1} \mathbf{1}^T$$ $$V_i^{-1} = \frac{1}{\sigma^2}\left(- \rho_i \mathbf{1}\mathbf{1}^T\right)$$ $$\log|V_i| = (n_i - 1) \log \sigma^2 + \log(\sigma^2 + n_i\sigma_b^2)$$ summands one-dimensional functions \(\rho\) client evaluates centralised aggregates (sum \(n_i \rho_i\) sum \(\log(\sigma^2 + n_i \sigma_b^2)\)); per-cluster residual sums \(\sum_{ij} r_{ij}\) returned outcome server single aggregate vector (one scalar per cluster) already-documented cluster-ID inter-server leakage tier. Inter-server disclosure: DCF peer learns cluster membership (class Cox event-time ordering). Absolute cluster sizes revealed; individual observations .

what: Federated linear mixed model via REML 1-D profile (K=3) — ds.vertLMM.k3
title: Federated linear mixed model via REML 1-D profile (K=3) — ds.vertLMM.k3

what: Federated multiple imputation with Rubin pooling — ds.vertMI
title: Federated multiple imputation with Rubin pooling — ds.vertMI
text: Fit GLM vertically partitioned DataSHIELD data multiple imputation missing values. Imputations stay server holding missing variable (via dsvertImputeColumnDS); client ever sees \(M\) pooled coefficient vectors covariance matrices applies Rubin's rules client-side. Protocol \(m = 1..M\): 1. every server holding column missingness, call dsvertImputeColumnDS seed \(s_m\). server draws local imputation using Bayesian-ridge model conditional complete-case columns available server. imputed column written back aligned data frame per-round name (e.g. __dsvert_imp_<var>_<m>). 2. Run ds.vertGLM imputed data, collect beta_m Cov(beta_m). 3. Client accumulates \((\beta_m, \mathrm{Cov}_m)\). Rubin's pooling rules applied client-side: $$\bar\beta = \frac{1}{M}\sum_m \beta_m$$ $$W = \frac{1}{M}\sum_m \mathrm{Cov}_m$$ $$B = \frac{1}{M-1} \sum_m (\beta_m-\bar\beta)(\beta_m-\bar\beta)^T$$ $$T = W + (1 + 1/M) B$$

what: Federated negative binomial regression with dispersion estimate — ds.vertNB
title: Federated negative binomial regression with dispersion estimate — ds.vertNB
text: Fit negative binomial GLM vertically partitioned DataSHIELD data. Two-stage: Fit \(\hat\beta\) via dsVert Poisson GLM (identical score canonical log link point estimate ; covariance differs). Estimate \(\hat\theta\) client-side Newton-Raphson NB profile log-likelihood, evaluated candidate \(\theta\) dsvertNBProfileSumsDS returns \(\sum\psi(y_i+\theta)\), \(\sum\psi_1(y_i+\theta)\), \(n\), \(\bar y\) scalar aggregates outcome server. Anscombe/Lawless score \(s(\theta)=\sum\psi(y_i+\theta)-n\psi(\theta)+n\log(\theta/(\bar y+\theta))\) zero MLE; derivative \(s'(\theta)=\sum\psi_1(y_i+\theta)-n\psi_1(\theta)+n[1/\theta-1/(\bar y+\theta)]\) supplies Newton step. Initial value comes method--moments \(\hat\theta_0=\bar y^2/(s_y^2-\bar y)\). Rescale Poisson SE \(\sqrt{1 + \bar y / \hat\theta}\) reported z-stats reflect NB variance inflation. aggregates scalar sums y; per-patient disclosure.

text: NB(mu_i, theta) log-likelihood score theta $$s(\theta) = \sum_i \psi(y_i + \theta) - n \psi(\theta)                      + n \log \theta - \sum_i \log(\mu_i + \theta).$$ iid-mu approximation used ds.vertNB replaces last term \(n \log(\bar y + \theta)\). homogeneous cohorts (small \(\text{Var}(\mu)\)) tight; regression-rich settings bias theta can reach ~16% (quine, overdispersed counts) relative MASS::glm.nb. first-order correction uses aggregate marginal variance y decomposed via NB law total variance: \(\text{Var}(y) = E[\mu] + E[\mu^2]/\theta + \text{Var}(\mu)\). \(\bar y\) \(s_y^2\) (scalar aggregates dsvertLocalMomentsDS) iid theta_0 seed, refine via Brent root-finding corrected score \(s_{\text{corr}}(\theta) = s_{\text{iid}}(\theta) - \frac{1}{2}         \frac{n \, \hat{V}_\mu}{(\bar y + \theta)^2}\) \(\hat V_\mu\) aggregate estimate \(\text{Var}(\mu)\) second term Taylor correction \(\sum_i \log(\mu_i + \theta)\) around \(\bar y\). aggregate \(\hat V_\mu\) computed \(\hat V_\mu = \max(0,\; s_y^2 - \bar y - \bar y^2 / \hat\theta_0)\) – portion total y variance explained NB conditional variance \(\mu + \mu^2/\theta\). quantities scalar aggregates; per-patient disclosure. Full-share-space Clenshaw evaluation \(\sum_i \log(\mu_i + \theta)\) using shipped Ring127LogShiftPlaintext Chebyshev primitive + DCF argument reduction stricter variant scheduled separately; first-order correction closes bulk iid-mu bias (quine: 16% -> 4-5%) without new MPC machinery.

text: ds.vertOrdinal object (among fields): thresholds \(\alpha_k\) (intercepts K-1 cumulative binomial fits) beta_po \(\gamma\) (BLUE-pooled slope coefficients K-1 fits). CUMULATIVE-BINOMIAL GLM convention, .e.\ fit form
code: \eqn{P(Y \leq k | X) = \mathrm{sigmoid}(\alpha_k + X^\top \gamma)},  NOT the \code{MASS::polr} convention \eqn{P(Y \leq k | X) = \mathrm{sigmoid}(\theta_k - X^\top \beta)}. The two agree under \eqn{\theta_k = \alpha_k} and \eqn{\beta = -\gamma}. Therefore a caller comparing against \code{coef(polr)} must flip the sign of \code{beta_po} (or equivalently evaluate predictions with \eqn{\mathrm{sigmoid}(\theta_k + X^\top \gamma)} on the \code{ds.vertOrdinal} outputs). Empirically the cumulative probabilities agree with polr to max \eqn{|\Delta P| \approx 5 \times 10^{-2}} on the housing subset once the convention is honoured (probe_ordinal_harness.R, 2026-04-21).

what: Federated joint proportional-odds ordinal regression via Ring127 MPC-orchestrated Newton iteration — ds.vertOrdinalJointNewton
title: Federated joint proportional-odds ordinal regression via Ring127 MPC-orchestrated Newton iteration — ds.vertOrdinalJointNewton
text: Full per-patient PO Newton. K-level ordered outcome PO log-likelihood $$\ell(\beta, \theta) = \sum_i \log[F(\theta_{y_i} - \eta_i)                                            - F(\theta_{y_i-1} - \eta_i)],$$ \(F = \mathrm{sigmoid}\), \(\eta_i = X_i \beta\), \(\theta_0 = -\infty\), \(\theta_K = +\infty\). Score: $$\partial \ell / \partial \beta_j = -\sum_i x_{ij} \cdot           \frac{f(\theta_{y_i}-\eta_i) - f(\theta_{y_i-1}-\eta_i)}                {F(\theta_{y_i}-\eta_i) - F(\theta_{y_i-1}-\eta_i)},$$ \(f(u) = F(u)(1-F(u))\). MPC pipeline per outer Newton iter: Compute \(\eta\) share via k2ComputeEtaShareDS. threshold \(k\): compute \(\theta_k - \eta\) share via affine-combine (plaintext \(\theta_k\) public). Apply exp + recip \(\theta_k - \eta\) -> \(F_k\) share (\(F(u) = 1/(1+\exp(-u)) = e^u/(1+e^u)\) – evaluated exp(u) * (1/(1+exp(u))) via existing primitives). \(f_k = F_k (1 - F_k)\) via Beaver vecmul. Per-patient residual numerator/denominator built indicator-weighted differences (done outcome server since holds \(y_i\) plaintext). .ring127_recip_round_keyed F-difference share. Beaver vecmul (f-diff) * (1/F-diff) -> \(T_i\) share. Beaver matvec \(X^\top T\) -> aggregate score \(\beta\). Client Newton stacked \((\beta, \theta)\) using already-available $joint_mle$covariance Fisher block.

title: Principal Component Analysis for Vertically Partitioned Data — ds.vertPCA
text: function performs PCA using correlation matrix obtained via Ring63 Beaver MPC (via ds.vertCor). approach : Compute privacy-preserving correlation matrix using ds.vertCor (reuse existing one via cor_result parameter) Perform eigen decomposition correlation matrix Extract loadings (eigenvectors) eigenvalues Since PCA standardized data equivalent eigen decomposition correlation matrix, gives correct loadings variance explained. Note scores: function return principal component scores computing scores require access raw data. need scores, need compute server using loadings aggregate results (separate operation).

title: K=2 Beaver L-BFGS Pipeline — k2-beaver-lbfgs-client
text: Secure K=2 GLM L-BFGS optimizer. Gaussian: identity link, constant Fisher, L-BFGS (2 rounds/iter). Binomial/Poisson: wide spline sigmoid/exp, L-BFGS (6 rounds/iter, Fisher).

what: K>=3 Ring63 DCF + Beaver Gradient Loop — k3-ring63-dcf-loop
title: K>=3 Ring63 DCF + Beaver Gradient Loop — k3-ring63-dcf-loop
text: Pure Ring63 protocol K>=3 binomial/Poisson GLM. Uses additive secret sharing features 2 DCF parties, DCF wide spline sigmoid/exp, Beaver matvec gradient. Pure Ring63 additive secret sharing.

title: K>=3 Ring63 DCF + Beaver Gradient Loop — k3-ring63-dcf-loop
text: Protocol: One-time: servers share features+y 2 DCF parties (Ring63 FP) Per iteration: DCF parties compute eta X_share*beta DCF wide spline DCF parties -> mu shares Beaver matvec DCF parties -> gradient shares Client aggregates Ring63 shares -> plaintext gradient Client L-BFGS update Requires: transport keys . CPK, Galois, RLK. Precision: ~1e-3 (DCF approximation).

title: dsVertClient (development version)
text: ds.vertCox — federated Cox proportional-hazards regression (reverse-cumsum reformulation, Allison 1982 / Andreux 2020). Supports left-truncation (tstart_col), stratification (strata_col), Path B damped fixed-Fisher refinement, ring = 63 | 127. Defaults Ring127 (5/5 STRICT Pima synthetic; ~2× faster Path B). ds.vertCox.k3 — Cox K=3 via Allison-1982 Poisson trick offset + 100 baseline dummies; L-BFGS step-damping + backtracking guard (CGD). ds.vertCoxDiscreteNonDisclosive — K=2 discrete-time pooled-logistic Cox Aliasgari-Blanton 2013 share-mask gating. ds.vertMultinom — K-class one-vs-rest multinomial logistic. ds.vertMultinomJoint — covariance-rescaled OVR (full_irls toggle). ds.vertMultinomJointNewton — full softmax Newton via Ring127 Chebyshev exp + reciprocal + Beaver vecmul per-patient eta_k / mu_k shares (paper §V.row, Bohning-bounded). ds.vertOrdinal — proportional-odds via cumulative-binomial BLUE pool + threshold correction (McCullagh-Agresti). ds.vertOrdinalJointNewton — Tutz 1990 §3.2 block-diagonal joint Newton McCullagh §2.5 closed-form H_θθ + post-Newton refinement; sub-noise close-well L2 K=2. ds.vertNB — negative binomial GLM via two-stage Poisson β + θ Newton-Raphson; joint = TRUE re-Poisson-fits theta-adjusted weights. ds.vertNBMoMTheta — closed-form Method--Moments theta (Anscombe 1950 / Saha-Paul 2005), psi-free. ds.vertNBFullRegTheta — first-order corrected full-regression theta Ring127 NR-LOG share-domain primitive (Goldschmidt 1964 + Pugh 2004); SUB-NOISE K=2 (105×–109× σ-probe ratio). ds.vertLMM — Laird-Ware GLS closed-form REML; supports random intercept + slopes via random_slopes; ring = ring127 pipeline STRICT closure dense Gram matrices. ds.vertLMM.k3 — REML 1-D profile LMM K=3. ds.vertGLMM — Laplace-approximated binomial GLMM (single random intercept; stretch deliverable, moment-matching variance recovery). ds.vertIPW — propensity-weighted two-stage GLM wrapper. ds.vertLASSOProximal — proper LASSO via client-side proximal-gradient (FISTA-accelerated default) normal equations; 4–10× tighter coefficient agreement vs post-hoc soft-threshold. ds.vertLASSO1Step + ds.vertLASSOCV — one-step quadratic-surrogate LASSO AIC / BIC / EBIC information-criterion λ selector. ds.vertGEE — generalised estimating equations (working exchangeable / AR1 correlation, sandwich SE). ds.vertMI — multiple-imputation wrapper Rubin pooling. ds.vertChisq — two-way contingency χ² via Beaver dot product one-hot shares. ds.vertDesc — descriptive aggregates (mean / SD / min / max / histogram-based quantiles). ds.vertCor — Pearson correlation (Ring63 Beaver). ds.vertContrast, ds.vertWald, ds.vertConfint, ds.vertLR — inferential scaffolding (multivariate Wald, likelihood-ratio, contrast tests; CI helper).
